class.name: AutoworkTest
class.experimental: Some methods may not be implemented and may be changed in future versions.
class.brief_description: Test base class for the built-in unit testing framework.
class.description:
The AutoworkTest class is the base class for defining unit tests in the Autowork framework.
Tests are typically implemented by extending this class and defining test methods that contain assertions. The framework automatically discovers and executes these methods based on naming conventions and configuration rules provided by [AutoworkCollector] and [AutoworkConfig].

method.name: _after_all
method.description: Method called after all tests.

method.name: _after_each
method.description: Method called after each test.

method.name: _before_all
method.description: Method called before all tests.

method.name: _before_each
method.description: Method called before each test.

method.name: add_child_autofree
method.description: Adds a child node to the test sandbox and schedules it for automatic deletion at the end of the test.

method.name: add_child_autoqfree
method.description: Adds a child node to the test sandbox and schedules it for automatic deletion at the end of the test.

method.name: assert_accessors
method.description: Asserts that the [param property] of [param object] has a getter method that returns [param default] by default and a setter method that correctly updates the property. The assertion calls the setter with [param set_to] and then verifies that the getter returns the new value. [codeblock] assert_accessors(player, "position", Vector2.ZERO, Vector2(200, 200)) [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_almost_eq
method.description: Asserts that [param got] is equal to [param expected] within a margin of error defined by [param error_margin]. [codeblock] assert_almost_eq(1.05, 1.0, 0.1, "The value is inside of the range [0.9, 1.1]") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_almost_ne
method.description: Asserts that [param got] is not equal to [param expected] within a margin of error defined by [param error_margin]. [codeblock] assert_almost_ne(0.5, 1.0, 0.1, "The value is outside of the range [0.9, 1.1]") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_between
method.description: Asserts that [param got] is between [param expect_low] and [param expect_high]. [codeblock] assert_between(5, 1, 10, "5 is between 1 and 10") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_called
method.description: Asserts that a method was called on a stubbed or spied object. [codeblock] assert_called(obj, "call_me_maybe", [1, 2]) [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_called_count
method.description: Asserts the spied method was called the expected number of times. [codeblock] assert_call_count(child_node, "get_parent", 1, [], "get_parent called once") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_connected
method.description: Asserts that the [param signal] from [param source] is connected to [param method_name] from [param target].

method.name: assert_dir_does_not_exist
method.description: Asserts that a directory at [param dir_path] does not exist. [codeblock] assert_dir_does_not_exist("res://void.txt") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_dir_exists
method.description: Asserts that a directory at [param dir_path] does exist. [codeblock] assert_dir_exists("res://scripts/player.gd") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_does_not_have
method.description: Asserts that [param variant] does not have the specified [param element]. [b]Note:[/b] only types of [Array], [Dictionary] and [String] are supported. [codeblock] assert_does_not_have({"a": 1, "b": 2}, "c", "Dict does not have key c") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_eq
method.description: Asserts that [param expected] is equal to [param got]. [codeblock] assert_eq(2 + 2, 4, "2 + 2 is equal to 4") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_eq_deep
method.description: This is similar to [method assert_eq]. When this assert fails with [Array] or [Dictionary] as parameters, it will log the difference between the params.

method.name: assert_exports
method.description: Asserts that [param object] exports a property with the name [param property] and a type of [param type].

method.name: assert_false
method.description: Asserts that [param condition] is false.

method.name: assert_file_does_not_exist
method.description: Asserts a file does not exist at the specified [param file_path]. [codeblock] assert_file_does_not_exist("res://this_does_not_exist.txt") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_file_empty
method.description: Asserts the specified file at [param file_path] is empty.

method.name: assert_file_exists
method.description: Asserts a file exist at the specified [param file_path]. [codeblock] assert_file__exist("res://scripts/player.gd") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_file_not_empty
method.description: Asserts the specified file at [param file_path] is not empty.

method.name: assert_freed
method.description: Asserts that the [param object] has been freed.

method.name: assert_ge
method.description: Asserts [param got] is greater than or equal to [param expected].

method.name: assert_gt
method.description: Asserts [param got] is greater than [param expected].

method.name: assert_gte
method.description: Alias of [method assert_ge].

method.name: assert_has
method.description: Asserts that [param variant] has the specified [param element]. [b]Note:[/b] only types of [Array], [Dictionary] and [String] are supported. [codeblock] assert_has([1, 2, 3], 2, "Array has 2") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_has_method
method.description: Asserts that the [param object] has [param method]. [codeblock] assert_has_method(self, "_ready") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_has_signal
method.description: Asserts that the [param object] has [param signal]. [codeblock] assert_has_signal(node2d, "draw", "Node2D has 'draw' signal") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_is
method.description: Asserts that [param object] extends [param class]. [codeblock] assert_is(player, "CharacterBody2D", "player is a CharacterBody2D") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_le
method.description: Asserts [param got] is less than or equal to [param expected].

method.name: assert_lt
method.description: Asserts [param got] is less than [param expected].

method.name: assert_lte
method.description: Alias of [method assert_le].

method.name: assert_ne
method.description: Asserts that [param got] is not equal to [param expected]. [codeblock] assert_ne("hello", "world") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_ne_deep
method.description: This is similar to [method assert_eq]. When this assert fails with [Array] or [Dictionary] as parameters, it will log the difference between the params.

method.name: assert_no_new_orphans
method.description: Asserts that no new orphaned instances were created since the test began.

method.name: assert_not_between
method.description: Asserts that [param got] is not between [param expect_low] and [param expect_high]. [codeblock] assert_not_between(5, 1, 10, "15 is not between 1 and 10") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_not_called
method.description: Asserts that a method was not called on a stubbed or spied object. [codeblock] assert_not_called(obj, "get_name") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_not_connected
method.description: Asserts that the [param signal] from [param source] is not connected to [param method_name] from [param target]. [codeblock] assert_not_connected(base, self, "child_exiting_tree", "fail_test") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_not_freed
method.description: Asserts that the [param object] has not been freed.

method.name: assert_not_null
method.description: Asserts that [param got] is not null.

method.name: assert_not_same
method.description: Asserts [param got] and [param expected] are not the same.

method.name: assert_not_typeof
method.description: Asserts that the type of [param variant] is not [param type]. [codeblock] assert_not_typeof("5", TYPE_INT) [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_null
method.description: Asserts that [param got] is null.

method.name: assert_property
method.description: Asserts that the [param object] has a setter and getter methods for the [param property]. It will then use the getter to check if [param default] is returned. Then use the setter with [param set_to] and verify the getter returns the same value. [codeblock] assert_property(player, "position", Vector2(0, 0), Vector2(100, 100)) [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_property_with_backing_variable
method.description: Assumes backing variable will be [code]_[param property][/code]. This will perform all the asserts of [method assert_property]. Then this will set the value through the setter and check the backing variable value. It will then reset through the setter and set the backing variable and check the getter.

method.name: assert_readonly_property
method.description: Asserts the [param property] is readonly by trying set it to [param new_value] and then checking if the value is the same as [param expected].

method.name: assert_same
method.description: Asserts [param got] and [param expected] are the same.

method.name: assert_set_property
method.description:
Sets the [param property] of [param object] to [param new_value], then checks if the property is set to [param expected]. [codeblock] var a = 0: set(new_value): a = new_value + 2
assert_set_property(self, "a", 2, 4) [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_signal_emit_count
method.description: Asserts that [param signal] was fired [param expected_count] times from [param object]. [codeblock] assert_signal_emit_count(node, "draw", 2, "Signal 'draw' was emitted twice") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_signal_emitted
method.description: Asserts that [param signal] was emitted by [param object]. [codeblock] assert_signal_emitted(player, "dead", "the player is dead") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_signal_emitted_with_parameters
method.description: Asserts that [param signal] was emitted by [param object] with [param expected_parameters]. An optional [param index] can be passed when a signal has fired more than once. The default is to retrieve the most recent emission of the signal. [codeblock] assert_signal_emitted_with_parameters(node, "child_entered_tree", [node], 0, "Signal parameters matched") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_signal_not_emitted
method.description: Asserts that [param signal] was not emitted by [param object].

method.name: assert_string_contains
method.description: Assert that the [String] [param got] contains [param expected]. [codeblock] assert_string_contains("Hello, World!", "World") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_string_ends_with
method.description: Assert that the [String] [param got] ends with [param expected]. [codeblock] assert_string_ends_with("Hello, World!", "!") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_string_starts_with
method.description: Assert that the [String] [param got] starts with [param expected]. [codeblock] assert_string_starts_with("Hello, World!", "H") [/codeblock]

method.name: assert_true
method.description: Asserts that [param condition] is true.

method.name: assert_typeof
method.description: Asserts that the type of [param variant] is [param type]. [codeblock] assert_typeof(5, TYPE_INT) [/codeblock]

method.name: autofree
method.description: Marks [param object] to be freed after the test finishes.

method.name: autoqfree
method.description: Marks [param node] to be freed after the test finishes.

method.name: clear_parameters
method.description: Clears all stored test parameters for the current test context.

method.name: create_double
method.description: Creates a test double (mock or stub) from the provided object, scene, or script.

method.name: double_resource
method.description: Creates a test double (mock/stub) of a specific resource, scene, or script.

method.name: double_singleton
method.description: Creates a test double (mock/stub) of a specific resource, scene, or script. [codeblock] double_singleton("FakeSingletonName") [/codeblock]

method.name: fail_test
method.description: Marks the test as failing. Same as a failing assert.

method.name: get_assert_count
method.description: Returns the total number of asserts this script has made up to this method call. Call in [method _after_all] to get total count for a script.

method.name: get_call_count
method.description: Returns the call count for a [param method] with optional [param args].

method.name: get_call_parameters
method.description: Get the parameters for a method call to a doubled object. By default it will return the most recent call.

method.name: get_current_parameter_index
method.description: Returns the index of the currently active parameter set for a parameterized test.

method.name: get_fail_count
method.description: Returns the total number of failing asserts this script has made up to this method call. Call in [method _after_all] to get total count for a script.

method.name: get_logger
method.description: Returns the logger instance associated with this test.

method.name: get_parameter_count
method.description: Returns the total number of parameter sets available for this parameterized test.

method.name: get_pass_count
method.description: Returns the total number of passing asserts this script has made up to this method call. Call in [method _after_all] to get total count for a script.

method.name: get_pending_count
method.description: Returns the total number of pending asserts this script has made up to this method call. Call in [method _after_all] to get total count for a script.

method.name: get_signal_emit_count
method.description: This will return the number of times a [param signal] was fired.

method.name: get_signal_parameters
method.description: Returns the parameters of any watched signal. This works the same way that [method assert_signal_emitted_with_parameters] does.

method.name: get_summary
method.description: Returns a structured summary of the current test execution results.

method.name: get_summary_text
method.description: Returns a human-readable summary of the current test execution results.

method.name: get_test_count
method.description: Returns the total number of tests in this script.

method.name: has_parameters
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if this test has one or more parameter sets defined.

method.name: p
method.description: Alias of [method print_log]

method.name: pass_test
method.description: Marks the test as passing. Same as a passing assert.

method.name: pending
method.description: Mark the current test as pending.

method.name: print_log
method.description: Prints a message to the Autowork logger console.

method.name: set_current_parameter_index
method.description: Sets the current parameter index for a parameterized test execution.

method.name: set_doubler
method.description: Sets the [AutoworkDoubler] instance used by this test.

method.name: set_logger
method.description: Sets the [AutoworkLogger] instance used by this test.

method.name: set_spy
method.description: Sets the [AutoworkSpy] instance used by this test.

method.name: set_stubber
method.description: Sets the [AutoworkStubber] instance used by this test.

method.name: simulate
method.description: Simulate a number of frames by calling [method Node._process] or [method Node._physics_process] on an object and all of its descendents. By default it will call both [method Node._process] and [method Node._physics_process]. If [param check_is_processing] is true, it will check if [param node] is can process, see [method Node.is_processing] and [method Node.is_physics_processing].

method.name: skip_if_engine_version_lt
method.description:
Skip test if the Blazium Engine version is less than [param version] ([code]"external_major.external_minor.external_patch"[/code]). If [param check_godot] is [code]true[/code], it will check the Godot Engine version instead ([code]"major.minor.patch"[/code]).
See also [method Engine.get_version_info].

method.name: skip_if_engine_version_ne
method.description:
Skip test if the Blazium Engine version different from [param version] ([code]"external_major.external_minor.external_patch"[/code]). If [param check_godot] is [code]true[/code], it will check the Godot Engine version instead ([code]"major.minor.patch"[/code]).
See also [method Engine.get_version_info].

method.name: skip_if_godot_version_lt
method.description:
Alias of [method skip_if_engine_version_lt]. Skip test if the Godot Engine version is less than [param version] ([code]"major.minor.patch"[/code]). This is an alias for [method skip_if_engine_version_lt].
See also [method Engine.get_version_info].

method.name: skip_if_godot_version_ne
method.description:
Alias of [method skip_if_engine_version_ne]. Skip test if the Godot Engine version different from [param version] ([code]"major.minor.patch"[/code]). This is an alias for [method skip_if_engine_version_ne].
See also [method Engine.get_version_info].

method.name: spy
method.description: Creates and returns a spy-wrapped version of the provided object.

method.name: stub
method.description: Creates a stub configuration for the given object and method.

method.name: use_parameters
method.description: Sets the parameter sets for a parameterized test.

method.name: wait_for_signal
wait_physics_frames
method.description: Will wait for a number of physics [param frames], this returns [signal frameout] after waiting all frames. [codeblock] await wait_physics_frames(2, "Wait physics frames completed") [/codeblock]

method.name: wait_process_frames
method.description: Will wait for a number of process [param frames], this returns [signal frameout] after waiting all frames. [codeblock] await wait_process_frames(2, "Wait process frames completed") [/codeblock]

method.name: wait_seconds
method.description: Will wait for [param seconds] to pass. [codeblock] await wait_seconds(2, "Wait 2s completed") [/codeblock]

method.name: wait_until
method.description: Will wait until [param callable] returns true for a [param max_wait] amount of seconds. This returns [signal timeout], the return value will be true if the callable returned true, false if [param max_wait] was reached.

method.name: wait_while
method.description: Will wait while [param callable] returns true for a [param max_wait] amount of seconds. This returns [signal timeout], the return value will be true if the callable returned false, false if [param max_wait] was reached.

method.name: watch_signal
method.description: Starts watching a specific signal emitted by the given object.

method.name: watch_signals
method.description: Starts watching all signals emitted by the given object.

signal.name: frameout
signal.description: Signal used by [method wait_process_frames] and [method wait_physics_frames].

signal.name: timeout
signal.description: Signal used by [method wait_while], [method wait_until] and [method wait_for_signal].

constants.constant: Call both [method Node._process] and [method Node._physics_process]. See [method simulate].
